summary.completion_notes: All 12 value specifications are complete with scales, meters, constraints, targets, and stakeholder mappings. Six critical defects related to target-constraint consistency require resolution before deployment.

value_specifications.ambition: Disruption signals must be reliably ingested from carrier operations feeds without loss or duplication during initial receipt
value_specifications.scale: % of disruption signals successfully ingested and stored immutably on first receipt from [Source System]

scale_parameters.time_period: Per calendar day or per event batch
scale_parameters.success_criterion: Raw event stored with metadata (source_id, raw_event_id, received_at, checksum) intact

value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-001: Automated log analysis via ELK + database integrity checks. Daily at 06:00 UTC.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-001b: Weekly manual audit with cryptographic hash verification (50 event sample)

record.holder: Kafka, Kinesis, RabbitMQ with guaranteed delivery

stakeholders: Carrier Operations Team (high priority, high power)
Platform Engineering / SRE Team (high priority, high power)
key_risks: Carrier operations feed intermittent connectivity (medium probability, high impact)
Message broker overload during peak disruptions (medium probability, high impact)
Checksum collision or calculation errors (low probability, medium impact)
Network partition events (medium probability, high impact)
value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects specific to this specification. Scale, meter, and targets are consistent.

value_specifications.ambition: Disruption events must be normalized from raw source format into canonical form with perfect accuracy and no data loss
value_specifications.scale: % of disruption events correctly normalized from [Source Format] to canonical form
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-002: Automated tests per 1000 events. Verify field mapping, type correctness, transformation accuracy. Per deployment via CI/CD.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-002b: Monthly manual validation of 20 events across different source formats
value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Constraints and targets aligned correctly.

value_specifications.ambition: Normalized disruption events must correlate to passenger bookings/Cases with high accuracy using deterministic matching strategies
value_specifications.scale: % of disruption events correctly correlated to the intended passenger Case using [Match Strategy] with match_score >= [Threshold]
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-003: For every 500 correlated events, measure accuracy. Store results, independently verify via manual QA review. Weekly audit.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-003b: Monthly integration test with 20 synthetic scenarios. Known PNR, ingest disruption, verify match_score >= 0.80.

record.holder: Mature airline revenue management systems with ML enrichment

value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Targets appropriately exceed constraints.

value_specifications.ambition: Passenger notifications must be sent within a target time window after disruption detection to enable passenger decision-making
value_specifications.scale: Minutes elapsed from disruption event effective_time_utc to notification dispatch completion (p95 end-to-end latency)
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-004: Continuous measurement via time-series database (Prometheus + Grafana). Record effective_time_utc to dispatch_timestamp for every notification. Daily compute p50, p95, p99 by event type and severity.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-004b: Synthetic transaction monitoring every 10 minutes in staging. Inject synthetic disruption, measure injection-to-dispatch latency.

record.holder: Highly optimized airline/rail systems (Lufthansa AirMessage, SNCF)

tolerable.notes: Upper bound/worst case for latency metrics where lower is better

value_specifications.owner: Platform Engineering Lead / Head of SRE
value_specifications.qc_notes: For latency metrics (lower is better): Constraint (45 min) should be interpreted as upper bound/worst case. Targets (10 min goal, 8 min wish, 2 min stretch) correctly achieve lower/faster values and DO exceed constraints. However, absolute constraint semantics may confuse stakeholders. Recommend documentation clarifying: 'For latency-type metrics, targets correctly exceed constraints by achieving faster notification delivery.'

value_specifications.ambition: The system must suppress duplicate notifications to the same passenger on the same channel using the same template, avoiding notification spam while preserving audit trails
value_specifications.scale: % of duplicate disruption signals correctly identified and suppressed (no duplicate notification sent) for the same [Case, Channel, Template] triple
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-005: Automated log analysis daily at 07:00 UTC. Query suppression logs, calculate Duplicate Prevention Rate and False Positive Suppression Rate. Real-time alerts for false positive rate > 0.5%.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-005b: Monthly integration test. Ingest same disruption signal 5 times. Verify 1 notification dispatched, timeline has 1 NotificationDispatched + 4 NotificationSuppressedAsDuplicate.

record.holder: Enterprise message platforms (AWS SQS, Kafka, Stripe)

value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Constraints and targets aligned correctly.

value_specifications.ambition: The system must maintain a complete, append-only, immutable timeline of all Case state transitions for regulatory compliance and operational auditing
value_specifications.scale: % of Cases with complete, append-only timeline entries for all required state transitions, stored immutably with timestamps
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-006: Automated database query daily at 08:00 UTC. For every Case at NotificationDispatched status, retrieve timeline and verify all 8 required entries present in order. Real-time alerting for missing entries (critical).
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-006b: Quarterly manual audit. Randomly select 30 Cases. Manually verify timeline completeness, chronological order, no missing entries, timestamp reasonableness.

record.holder: Financial transaction systems, healthcare audit logs, regulatory-required trails

value_specifications.qc_notes: Goal (99.5%) equals Constraint (Tolerable: 99.5%); gap = 0%. Planguage requires targets to exceed constraints. RECOMMENDATION: Increase goal to 99.7% or higher to create separation between survival threshold (Tolerable) and committed achievement (Goal).

value_specifications.ambition: Raw disruption events and all derived data must be stored immutably with cryptographic integrity verification to prevent tampering or data loss
value_specifications.scale: % of stored raw events and Case records with verified cryptographic integrity (checksum, hash, or signature) intact after storage
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-007: Automated hash verification job daily at 03:00 UTC. Retrieve all records, recompute SHA-256 hashes, compare computed vs. stored. Flag mismatches as critical integrity violations.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-007b: Semi-annual penetration test (June, December). Security team attempts to modify stored records. Verify modifications prevented at storage layer or detected by hash verification.

record.holder: Cryptographically-secured systems (certificate authorities, regulated financial systems)

value_specifications.owner: Platform Security Lead / Chief Information Security Officer
value_specifications.qc_notes: Goal (99.9%) equals Constraint (Tolerable: 99.9%); gap = 0%. Targets must exceed constraints. RECOMMENDATION: Increase goal to 99.95% or higher to create separation.

value_specifications.ambition: Passenger notifications must respect consent, locale, quiet hours rules, and comply with regulatory requirements (GDPR, airline regulations, TCPA)
value_specifications.scale: % of outbound notifications that comply with [Compliance Rule] without violating [Regulation]
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-008: Automated compliance checks embedded in dispatch logic. Before dispatch: verify consent_state='opt_in', check quiet hours, verify locale match. Log all results. Daily compute Compliance Rate. Real-time alerts for opt_out violations.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-008b: Monthly compliance audit. Randomly sample 100 notifications. Manually verify consent, quiet hours compliance, locale match. Prepare compliance report for legal.

status.source: Estimated based on consent and quiet hours checks

record.holder: Enterprise marketing automation (Gmail, Mailchimp, Twilio) with GDPR/TCPA compliance

value_specifications.qc_notes: Goal (99.7%) equals Constraint (Tolerable: 99.7%); gap = 0%. Targets must exceed constraints per Planguage principle. RECOMMENDATION: Increase goal to 99.85% or higher to exceed constraint threshold and provide regulatory safety margin.

value_specifications.ambition: The system must process disruption events and dispatch notifications at sufficient scale to handle peak loads without queueing delays or message loss
value_specifications.scale: Events processed per minute (throughput) and p95 end-to-end latency from event ingest to notification dispatch
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-009: Continuous measurement via time-series database (Prometheus/InfluxDB). Emit metrics at each pipeline stage. Calculate rolling 5-min and 1-hour averages. Measure p50, p95, p99 per stage and end-to-end. Daily aggregate report at 07:00 UTC.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-009b: Monthly load testing in staging (10x peak = 1000 events/min for 30 minutes). Target: >= 1000 events/min sustained, p95 < 30 min, error rate = 0%, recovery < 5 min.

record.holder: Large-scale airline/rail networks (Lufthansa, SNCF) with distributed stream processing

value_specifications.owner: Platform Engineering Lead / Head of Infrastructure
value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Targets appropriately exceed constraints.

value_specifications.ambition: Passenger notifications must contain clear, actionable information including what changed, new expected time, and next steps to enable informed decision-making
value_specifications.scale: % of passenger notifications containing all required information elements: what changed, new expected time, next steps/call-to-action, Case reference
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-010: Automated content validation at composition time. For every notification: verify presence of 4 elements (parse text for keywords). Measure Flesch-Kincaid grade level (target: <= 8). Daily compute Completeness Rate and Clarity Rate.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-010b: Monthly user comprehension test. Recruit 10-15 representative passengers. Show 5 sample notifications (anonymized). Ask: What happened? When is new time? What next? How clear? Calculate comprehension success and average clarity rating.

record.holder: Well-designed notification systems with A/B testing and UX optimization

value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Constraints and targets aligned correctly.

value_specifications.ambition: Secure deep links to Case details must expire after a defined period and be revocable to prevent unauthorized access and ticket abuse
value_specifications.scale: % of deep links that are revoked or expired after [Expiration Period] minutes and are non-functional before expiration only if explicitly revoked
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-011: Automated link lifecycle testing. Generate synthetic links continuously. Test 3 scenarios: (a) access before expiration (200 OK), (b) access after 120 min (401/403), (c) revoke then access (401/403). Daily compute Expiration Enforcement Rate, Unauthorized Access Prevention Rate, Revocation Functionality Rate.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-011b: Quarterly penetration test (Mar, Jun, Sep, Dec). Security team attempts to bypass expiration/revocation controls. Verify all bypasses fail. Document successful bypasses as critical defects.

status.source: Estimated based on cryptographic deep link implementation

record.holder: High-security systems with cryptographic token management (OAuth, payment processors)

value_specifications.owner: Platform Security Lead / Chief Information Security Officer
value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Targets appropriately exceed constraints.

value_specifications.ambition: The system must support versioning of event schemas, templates, and severity rules to enable safe evolution without breaking existing workflows
value_specifications.scale: % of Cases and notifications correctly using the specified [Schema Version] / [Template Version] / [Ruleset Version] without compatibility errors
value_specifications.meter_primary: METER-012: Automated version compliance check daily at 06:00 UTC. Query all Cases and notifications. Verify 100% have version field populated, version exists in registry. Compute Version Tracking Compliance. Real-time alerts for missing version info.
value_specifications.meter_secondary: METER-012b: Quarterly backward compatibility test. Before deploying new schema/template/rule version, extract 50 old Cases (6 months ago). Reprocess using new version. Compare results against original values. Flag divergence as compatibility issue.

status.source: Estimated based on version field tracking

record.holder: Data systems with comprehensive schema versioning (Avro, Protobuf, JSON Schema)

value_specifications.qc_notes: No defects. Constraints and targets aligned correctly.

scales.scale: % of disruption signals successfully ingested and stored immutably on first receipt from [Source System]

scale_parameters.time_period: Per calendar day or per event batch
scale_parameters.success_criterion: Raw event stored with metadata intact

meters.measurement_tool: Automated log analysis via ELK stack + database integrity checks
meters.measurement_frequency: Daily at 06:00 UTC, real-time monitoring dashboard
meters.measurement_owner: Data Engineering (ingest pipeline owner) with QA spot-checks

benchmarks.record_holder: Top-tier event streaming platforms (Kafka, Kinesis, RabbitMQ)
benchmarks.record_source: Industry standard for message brokers with durability guarantees

constraints.reasoning: Below 99%, system losing disruption information that passengers depend on

primary_stakeholders: Platform Engineering / SRE Team (high priority, high power)
Carrier Operations Team (high priority, high power)
secondary_stakeholders: IT Operations / Incident Response (high priority, medium power)

qc_findings.defect_description: Goal (99.5%) equals Constraint (99.5%); gap = 0%. Targets must exceed constraints per Planguage principle.
qc_findings.fix_recommendation: Increase goal to 99.7% or higher to exceed constraint threshold and create separation between survival (Tolerable) and commitment (Goal).

qc_findings.defect_description: Goal (99.9%) equals Constraint (99.9%); gap = 0%. No separation between minimum acceptable and committed achievement.
qc_findings.fix_recommendation: Increase goal to 99.95% or higher to create meaningful gap above constraint.

qc_findings.defect_description: Goal (99.7%) equals Constraint (99.7%); gap = 0%. Regulatory requirement with no safety margin.
qc_findings.fix_recommendation: Increase goal to 99.85% or higher to exceed constraint and provide buffer for regulatory compliance.

qc_findings.defect_description: For latency metrics (lower is better): Constraint = 45 min (worse/upper bound), Goal = 10 min (better/lower). This is correct mathematically but semantics may confuse stakeholders unfamiliar with Planguage.
qc_findings.fix_recommendation: Add documentation: 'For latency-type metrics, lower values represent better performance. Targets (10 min goal, 8 min wish, 2 min stretch) correctly exceed Constraint (45 min) by achieving faster notification delivery.' Clarify that constraint represents upper acceptable bound, targets represent better achievement within that bound and beyond.

quality_assessment.exit_notes: Do not deploy. Specification is 100% complete but 6 critical defects require resolution. Estimated rework time: 2-3 hours. Primary issues: (1) Three specifications have goal = tolerable (no separation); increase goals per Planguage principle. (2) Latency metrics need semantic documentation. (3) Overall specification is high-quality and comprehensive; defects are fixable within scope.
